meaning: n.牛蝇,形似苍蝇,讨厌的人
examples: FOR a week the Gadfly lay in a fearful state.
No matter I live, or I die, I was a gadfly, happy flying.
After 13 years, an ugly man called Gadfly returned to Italy.
His departure was first reported on the site started by longtime Shell gadfly Alfred Donovan.
Cold comfort to Rep. Edward Markey (D-Mass.), a BP gadfly since the blowout began.
